claims.claim: 1MDB Asset Recovery Has Reached Over RM20.7 Billion
claims.source: The Edge Malaysia, citing MOF written parliamentary reply, Oct 14-15 2025: https://theedgemalaysia.com/node/774093; Bernama citing MOF written parliamentary reply, Nov 27 2025: https://bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2496226
claims.correction: As a narrow claim about settlements with Goldman Sachs, AmBank and JPMorgan, about RM20.73 billion is supported. As a headline about total 1MDB/SRC recovery, it is stale and understated: MOF later reported RM31.2 billion recovered up to Sept 30, 2025, and MACC chief Azam Baki later said RM31.3 billion of 1MDB funds had been recovered as of 2025.
claims.stale_check: The analysis is dated 2026-05-17 but uses an October 2025 figure; later MOF/Bernama reporting on Nov 27 2025 and MACC/Bernama reporting on May 6 2026 give higher totals.

claims.claim: The Ministry of Finance reported in October 2025 that Malaysia had recovered more than RM20.73 billion through settlements with financial institutions involved in the 1MDB scandal
claims.source: The Edge Malaysia, Oct 14 2025, quoting MOF written reply to Yeo Bee Yin: https://theedgemalaysia.com/node/774093; MOF JPMorgan press release, Aug 22 2025: https://www.mof.gov.my/portal/en/news/press-release/the-malaysian-government-and-jpmorganchase-resolve-all-historic-and-future-matters-related-to-1malaysia-development-bhd-1mdb; MOF AmBank citation, Feb 26 2021: https://www.mof.gov.my/portal/en/news/press-citations/ammb-to-pay-rm2-83-bln-to-malaysian-govt-for-1mdb-related-settlement; MOF Goldman statement, July 24 2020: https://www.mof.gov.my/portal/ms/berita/akhbar/kenyataan-media-kementerian-kewangan-berhubung-penyelesaian-kerajaan-malaysia-dengan-goldman-sachs
claims.correction: Use 'about RM20.73 billion from three financial-institution settlements' and cite the component settlements. The exact parliamentary written reply was not directly retrieved, so confidence is limited.
claims.stale_check: Later total asset recovery figures exceeded this narrow settlement figure.

claims.claim: substantially more than earlier official tallies suggested
claims.source: MOF March 3 2022 citation said 1MDB-related funds recovered as of Jan 31 2022 amounted to RM19.14 billion: https://www.mof.gov.my/portal/en/news/press-citations/1mdb-related-funds-totalling-rm19-14-bln-recovered-as-of-jan-31-2022-finance-ministry; MOF/Bernama Nov 27 2025 says RM31.2 billion recovered related to 1MDB and SRC: https://bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2496226
claims.correction: Specify which earlier tally is being compared. RM20.73 billion is only modestly above RM19.14 billion, while RM31.2 billion is substantially above it.
claims.stale_check: No stale issue; comparison depends on baseline.

claims.claim: 1MDB asset recovery has surpassed RM20.7 billion.
claims.source: MOF/Bernama, Nov 27 2025: https://bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2496226; The Edge Malaysia, Oct 14 2025: https://theedgemalaysia.com/node/774093
claims.correction: If referring to total recovery, use RM31.2 billion related to 1MDB and SRC as of Sept 30, 2025. If referring only to financial-institution settlements, say that explicitly.
claims.stale_check: Stale by Nov 27 2025 and again by May 6 2026.

claims.source: The Edge Malaysia, Oct 14 2025, citing MOF written parliamentary reply: https://theedgemalaysia.com/node/774093
claims.correction: The figure should be described as an MOF written parliamentary reply reported on Oct 14-15, 2025, not necessarily a standalone MOF press release.
claims.stale_check: Later figures superseded it for total recovery.

claims.claim: The Goldman Sachs 2020 settlement alone returned USD3.9 billion to Malaysia.
claims.source: MOF Goldman settlement statement, July 24 2020: https://www.mof.gov.my/portal/ms/berita/akhbar/kenyataan-media-kementerian-kewangan-berhubung-penyelesaian-kerajaan-malaysia-dengan-goldman-sachs; Goldman Sachs SEC filing, Oct 2020: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/769993/000119312520274177/d75479d40app.htm
claims.correction: Goldman Sachs agreed to a USD3.9 billion settlement comprising USD2.5 billion cash and a guarantee that Malaysia would receive at least USD1.4 billion in proceeds from 1MDB-related assets seized by authorities. Saying it 'returned USD3.9 billion' overstates the cash return.
claims.stale_check: No stale issue; this is a wording/scope problem.

claims.claim: Goldman Sachs paid USD3.9 billion in 2020, the largest single 1MDB-related recovery to date.
claims.source: MOF Goldman settlement statement, July 24 2020: https://www.mof.gov.my/portal/ms/berita/akhbar/kenyataan-media-kementerian-kewangan-berhubung-penyelesaian-kerajaan-malaysia-dengan-goldman-sachs; MOF AmBank settlement, Feb 26 2021: https://www.mof.gov.my/portal/en/news/press-citations/ammb-to-pay-rm2-83-bln-to-malaysian-govt-for-1mdb-related-settlement; MOF JPMorgan settlement, Aug 22 2025: https://www.mof.gov.my/portal/en/news/press-release/the-malaysian-government-and-jpmorganchase-resolve-all-historic-and-future-matters-related-to-1malaysia-development-bhd-1mdb
claims.correction: Use 'Goldman Sachs agreed to a USD3.9 billion settlement in 2020, including USD2.5 billion cash and a USD1.4 billion asset-recovery guarantee.' The 'largest single recovery' claim is plausible among named settlements but was not confirmed from an exhaustive primary list.

claims.claim: The settlement included a USD2.5 billion cash payment plus a guarantee to help recover at least USD1.4 billion in misappropriated assets.
claims.source: MOF Goldman settlement statement, July 24 2020: https://www.mof.gov.my/portal/ms/berita/akhbar/kenyataan-media-kementerian-kewangan-berhubung-penyelesaian-kerajaan-malaysia-dengan-goldman-sachs; Goldman Sachs SEC filing, Oct 2020: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/769993/000119312520274177/d75479d40app.htm
claims.correction: No correction needed, except 'guarantee that Malaysia would receive at least USD1.4 billion in proceeds' is more precise than 'help recover'.

claims.claim: Recovery flows from settlements with financial institutions, asset seizures across multiple jurisdictions, and criminal prosecutions.
claims.source: US DOJ Dec 2024 release describes recoveries and international assistance: https://www.justice.gov/archives/opa/pr/justice-department-recovers-additional-20m-misappropriated-1mdb-funds; FATF/APG Malaysia Mutual Evaluation 2025, Box 8.1: https://www.apgml.org/sites/default/files/2025-12/Malaysia%20MER%202025.pdf; MOF civil suits citation, May 10 2021: https://www.mof.gov.my/portal/en/news/press-citations/mof-civil-suits-filed-by-1mdb-src-to-recover-over-rm96-6-bln-in-assets
claims.correction: Settlements and foreign civil forfeiture/seizure are well supported. 'Criminal prosecutions' should be phrased more cautiously unless tied to a specific forfeiture, confiscation order, or conviction-linked recovery.

claims.claim: The US Department of Justice has separately recovered and returned about USD1.4 billion in assets to Malaysia by mid-2024.
claims.source: US DOJ, June 13 2024: https://www.justice.gov/archives/opa/pr/justice-department-repatriates-14b-misappropriated-1mdb-funds-malaysia; US DOJ, Dec 2024: https://www.justice.gov/archives/opa/pr/justice-department-recovers-additional-20m-misappropriated-1mdb-funds
claims.correction: Use 'repatriated approximately USD1.4 billion' or 'recovered, returned and assisted in returning approximately USD1.4 billion' to match DOJ wording.
claims.stale_check: DOJ announced an additional USD20 million recovery in Dec 2024, but still described the June 2024 total as approximately USD1.4 billion.

claims.source: MOF March 3 2022 citation states actual 1MDB losses could not be verified: https://www.mof.gov.my/portal/en/news/press-citations/1mdb-related-funds-totalling-rm19-14-bln-recovered-as-of-jan-31-2022-finance-ministry; US DOJ Dec 2024 alleges more than USD4.5 billion misappropriated: https://www.justice.gov/archives/opa/pr/justice-department-recovers-additional-20m-misappropriated-1mdb-funds
claims.stale_check: Later MACC statements cite RM42 billion misappropriated, but there are still different baselines: misappropriated funds, debt obligations, civil claims and recoverable assets.

claims.claim: Figures cited range from USD4.5 billion (DOJ) up to RM30+ billion in broader Malaysian estimates.
claims.source: US DOJ Dec 2024: https://www.justice.gov/archives/opa/pr/justice-department-recovers-additional-20m-misappropriated-1mdb-funds; Bernama quoting MACC chief Azam Baki, May 6 2026: https://bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2553395; MOF/Bernama Nov 27 2025: https://bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2496226
claims.correction: DOJ says more than USD4.5 billion was misappropriated. Malaysian figures are now commonly stated as RM42 billion misappropriated by MACC, RM31.2 billion recovered related to 1MDB/SRC by MOF as of Sept 30 2025, and RM31.3 billion 1MDB recovery by MACC as of 2025. 'RM30+ billion in broader Malaysian estimates' is vague and mixes loss and recovery baselines.
claims.stale_check: The RM30+ phrasing is stale/incomplete after May 2026 MACC statements citing RM42 billion misappropriated and RM31.3 billion recovered.

claims.claim: Recovery comparisons depend on which loss baseline is used.
claims.source: MOF March 3 2022 on unverifiable actual losses: https://www.mof.gov.my/portal/en/news/press-citations/1mdb-related-funds-totalling-rm19-14-bln-recovered-as-of-jan-31-2022-finance-ministry; US DOJ Dec 2024: https://www.justice.gov/archives/opa/pr/justice-department-recovers-additional-20m-misappropriated-1mdb-funds; MOF/Bernama Nov 27 2025: https://bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2496226

claims.claim: Multiple jurisdictions have ongoing litigation that may produce further recoveries.
claims.source: US DOJ Dec 2024 notes international recovery work and additional recovery agreement: https://www.justice.gov/archives/opa/pr/justice-department-recovers-additional-20m-misappropriated-1mdb-funds; Bernama Nov 11 2025 quotes Anwar saying recovery continues through a special task force handling cases in Switzerland and London: https://www.bernama.com/en/region/news.php?id=2489434; The Edge Oct 14 2025 reports Goldman LCIA arbitration and 1MDB suits against other institutions: https://theedgemalaysia.com/node/774093
claims.correction: No correction needed, but stronger if specific jurisdictions and case types are named.

claims.claim: The recovery story is no longer 'how little came back'. It is whether the recovered funds reach public accounts and reform follows.
claims.source: The Edge Oct 14 2025 says all proceeds from the three settlements were deposited into the MOF-managed Asset Recovery Trust Account: https://theedgemalaysia.com/node/774093; MOF/Bernama Nov 27 2025 says AA MKA funds are regulated and used only for 1MDB/SRC debts and commitments: https://bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2496226
claims.correction: For the named financial-institution settlements, available reporting says funds were fully received and deposited into the Asset Recovery Trust Account. The unresolved issue is less whether they reach a public account and more how they are used, audited, and whether institutional reforms follow.

claims.claim: RM20.7 billion is the largest financial-crime recovery in Malaysian history.
claims.source: No primary source found establishing a historical ranking for Malaysian financial-crime recoveries. Bernama/MACC May 6 2026 says RM31.3 billion is among the highest asset recovery rates globally for large-scale financial crime cases: https://bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2553395
claims.correction: Replace with a sourced formulation: 'MACC chief Azam Baki said the RM31.3 billion recovered as of 2025 represented 74.5% of RM42 billion and ranked among the highest asset-recovery rates globally for large-scale financial crime cases.'
claims.stale_check: Stale and under-specified: later public figure is RM31.3 billion, not RM20.7 billion.

claims.claim: The remaining test is whether the institutions that enabled the scandal have been structurally changed.
claims.source: FATF/APG Malaysia Mutual Evaluation 2025 identifies improvements and remaining AML/CFT effectiveness issues, but does not directly verify this normative claim: https://www.apgml.org/sites/default/files/2025-12/Malaysia%20MER%202025.pdf
claims.correction: Frame as analysis/opinion, or support it with specific institutional reform evidence from Bank Negara, Securities Commission, MACC, AGC, Parliament, or FATF/APG findings.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: The Goldman Sachs 2020 settlement alone returned USD3.9 billion to Malaysia.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: The USD3.9 billion figure was a settlement value comprising USD2.5 billion cash plus a USD1.4 billion guarantee tied to proceeds from seized assets. 'Returned' implies the whole USD3.9 billion was paid or repatriated as cash.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: 1MDB Asset Recovery Has Reached Over RM20.7 Billion
cardinal_sin_flags.why: The figure is accurate only for selected financial-institution settlements. By Sept 30 2025, MOF reported RM31.2 billion recovered related to 1MDB and SRC, so the headline understates total recovery unless the settlement scope is explicit.
cardinal_sin_flags.primary_source: Bernama citing MOF written parliamentary reply, Nov 27 2025: https://bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2496226

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: 1MDB asset recovery has surpassed RM20.7 billion.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: As of later official reporting, recovered funds were materially higher: RM31.2 billion related to 1MDB/SRC as of Sept 30 2025, and MACC later stated RM31.3 billion of 1MDB funds had been recovered as of 2025.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: RM20.7 billion is the largest financial-crime recovery in Malaysian history.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: No primary source was found that ranks RM20.7 billion as the largest financial-crime recovery in Malaysian history. A later MACC statement supports a different, narrower claim about RM31.3 billion being among the highest global recovery rates.
cardinal_sin_flags.primary_source: Bernama quoting MACC chief Azam Baki, May 6 2026: https://bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2553395

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Figures cited range from USD4.5 billion (DOJ) up to RM30+ billion in broader Malaysian estimates.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: The claim mixes different denominators: DOJ's misappropriation allegation, Malaysian recovery totals, Malaysian debt obligations and civil-claim amounts. MACC's later figure is RM42 billion misappropriated, not merely RM30+ billion.

omitted_facts: MOF reported that from creation of the Asset Recovery Trust Account in December 2018 to Sept 30 2025, RM31.2 billion including proceeds and foreign-exchange adjustments had been recovered related to 1MDB and SRC, and that the funds are used only for debt repayment and existing 1MDB/SRC commitments: https://bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2496226

MOF reported remaining AA MKA funds of RM5.29 billion and outstanding 1MDB debt obligations of RM24.46 billion as of Sept 30 2025, important context for whether recoveries cover liabilities: https://bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2496226
MACC chief Azam Baki said on May 6 2026 that Malaysia had recovered RM31.3 billion, or 74.5% of RM42 billion misappropriated from 1MDB, as of 2025: https://bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2553395

The Goldman Sachs settlement is under dispute: Goldman initiated LCIA arbitration on Oct 11 2023 over obligations under the Aug 18 2020 settlement agreement, according to MOF reporting: https://theedgemalaysia.com/node/774093

The US DOJ announced in Dec 2024 an additional USD20 million recovery and reiterated that it had recovered, returned and assisted in returning approximately USD1.4 billion to Malaysia: https://www.justice.gov/archives/opa/pr/justice-department-recovers-additional-20m-misappropriated-1mdb-funds
source_assessment: The original analysis cites only two T4A related issues, which are circular and not primary sources. It does not cite MOF press releases, parliamentary written replies, DOJ releases, FATF/APG evaluation material, court records, regulator filings, or company settlement disclosures. The institutional range in the original sourcing is therefore essentially absent: no government primary documents, no court texts, no regulator documents, no opposition or civil society view, no financial institution perspective, and no independent audit or academic source. The factual basis can be reconstructed from MOF, DOJ, FATF/APG, SEC and Bernama/The Edge reporting, but that work is not visible in the original analysis.
overall_assessment: The core direction of the analysis is broadly right: Malaysia has recovered very large sums related to 1MDB, the Goldman settlement was a major component, DOJ recoveries are about USD1.4 billion, and loss baselines vary. The main reliability problems are imprecise scope and stale framing. RM20.7 billion is best understood as selected financial-institution settlements, not total asset recovery; later official figures put total recovery around RM31.2-RM31.3 billion. The Goldman claim also overstates by saying USD3.9 billion was 'returned' or 'paid' when the primary documents describe USD2.5 billion cash plus a USD1.4 billion asset-recovery guarantee.
